name: Flask-Pypi-Proxy
license: BSD (setup.py license='BSD' and OSI 'BSD License' classifier; clause variant unspecified, no LICENSE file in repo)
last_updated: 2018-11-28 (repo archived read-only by owner; last PyPI release 0.5.1 on 2014-01-08)
perf_notes: No published benchmarks. Runs as a plain Flask WSGI app (single dev server by default). The download path reads the entire artifact into memory (open().read() then make_response) before responding, so large wheels are memory-heavy. Upstream simple/index pages are not cached at all — every /simple/<pkg>/ request for a non-private package re-fetches and re-parses the upstream PyPI page with BeautifulSoup. There is no cache size limit or eviction.
scale_notes: Single-node by design. No database, no clustering, no HA. State is the local filesystem cache (BASE_FOLDER_PATH); multiple workers could in theory share a filesystem but artifact writes are non-atomic (direct open()/write() and file.save(), no tmp+rename), so concurrent caching is racy. Not built for horizontal scaling. Development Status: Alpha.
summary: An archived, Python 2-era Flask app that acts as a lazy pull-through PyPI cache and a minimal unauthenticated host for private packages, storing eggs/sdists/wheels on the local filesystem with md5 sidecar files. Repo archived read-only since Nov 2018 and runs only on Python 2.6/2.7.
extras: PRIVATE_EGGS allowlist: names configured as private are always served from the local filesystem and never proxied upstream to PyPI — a manual dependency-confusion mitigation
Stores an md5 digest per artifact in a '.md5' sidecar file next to each cached/uploaded file; serves md5 via #md5= fragments in the simple HTML
Legacy unauthenticated upload at POST /pypi/ compatible with 'setup.py register'/'setup.py sdist upload' (reads name, content file, md5_digest form fields)
On-demand proxy crawls external/download links from upstream simple pages (BeautifulSoup), including SourceForge/Google Code style redirects, via a per-request ?remote= URL
SHOULD_USE_EXISTING mode prefers any locally cached package over re-fetching from upstream
HEAD support with python-magic MIME-type detection

Config via JSON file (FLASK_PYPI_PROXY_CONFIG) or env vars (PYPI_PROXY_BASE_FOLDER_PATH, PYPI_PROXY_LOGGING_PATH, PYPI_PROXY_PYPI_URL, PYPI_PROXY_PRIVATE_EGGS, PYPI_PROXY_SHOULD_USE_EXISTING, PYPI_PROXY_LOGGING_LEVEL)
No authentication of any kind on read, upload, or admin paths; everything is anonymous and writable
Simple index pages are generated from an HTML template with no PEP 503 name normalization (predates PEP 503); no JSON simple API
Python 2.6/2.7 only (uses urllib/urlparse, werkzeug.secure_filename); will not run on Python 3 unmodified
Repo archived read-only by the owner on 2018-11-28; last PyPI release (0.5.1) was 2014-01-08; effectively defunct
